data.title: CATL surpasses Kweichow Moutai to become China’s third-largest stock by market value
data.description:
• Contemporary Amperex Technology (CATL) overtook liquor maker Kweichow Moutai on Thursday to become China’s third-largest locally listed stock by market value, driven by a strong outlook for battery demand.
  • The Shenzhen-listed battery giant gained as much as 6% Thursday to a record high, pushing the company’s market capitalization to 1.83 trillion yuan ($257 billion), surpassing Moutai’s 1.8 trillion yuan valuation, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.
  • “Battery outlook is good,” and some investors may be rotating out of electric vehicle stocks into battery stocks, benefiting sector leader CATL, Kelvin Lau, an analyst at Daiwa Capital Markets Hong Kong, said.
  • CATL, the world’s largest electric vehicle battery manufacturer, saw its shares surge double digits on their Hong Kong debut in May following a record $4.6 billion IPO, boosted by high interest in EV battery suppliers.

data.title: Verbio SE reports FY results
data.description:
• Verbio SE press release (OTCPK:VBVBF): FY Revenue of €1.58B (-5.4% Y/Y).
  • Verbio confirms preliminary results with EBITDA of €14.2 million.
  • The increase in bioethanol and biomethane was primarily attributable to the plant in Nevada, Iowa (USA), whose production has risen continuously since the beginning of the year. The CO2 savings potential of the products produced and traded by Verbio also increased to 5.5 million tons of CO2 equivalent (FY 2023/24: 4.4 million tons).
  • The Executive Board expects EBITDA in the high double-digit million range for the 2025/26 fiscal year, significantly higher than the previous year.

data.title: BP launches £1.6 billion pension risk transfer deal with UK insurer L&G
data.description:
• UK insurer Legal and General Assurance Society on Thursday announced the completion of a £1.6 billion buy-in with the BP Pension Fund. 
  • Legal & General Group will manage risks for £1.6 billion ($2.2 billion) of BP’s (NYSE:BP) £18 billion fund, according to a statement on Thursday. 
  • The pension fund is among the UK’s largest and run by BP’s in-house asset manager.

data.title: KWS SAAT SE reports FY result
data.description:
• KWS SAAT SE & Co. KGaA (OTCPK:KNKZF): FY GAAP EPS of €4.24.
  • Revenue of €1.68B (flat Y/Y).

data.title: Nscale raises $1.1B to fuel AI data center expansion
data.description:
Nscale, a developer of artificial intelligence data centers, has secured $1.1 billion in financing just a week after announcing a partnership with Nvidia (NVDA) and OpenAI in the U.K.

The funding round was led by Aker ASA (OTCPK:AKAAF) and included participation from existing investors such as Point72, Nvidia (NVDA), Dell (DELL) and Nokia (NOK), Nscale said Thursday.

The raise follows the recent announcement of Nscale’s UK AI infrastructure commitment. Together with Microsoft (MSFT), the firm announced its plans to develop the UK’s largest AI supercomputer on its campus in Loughton.

Separately, Nscale, OpenAI and Nvidia announced they are together establishing Stargate UK, an infrastructure platform designed to deploy OpenAI’s technology in the country, with a particular focus on sovereign workloads.

In July, Aker and Nscale announced plans to build a data center in Norway, with OpenAI as an anchor customer. Looking ahead, Nscale plans to expand its relationship with Dell Technologies (DELL), using Dell AI infrastructure to further support sovereign AI efforts in Europe.

data.title: OCI N.V. reports H1 results
data.description:
• OCI N.V. press release (OTCPK:OCINF): Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.63.
  • Revenue of $1.06B (-56.6% Y/Y).

data.title: Is Circle considering 'reversible' stablecoin transactions? President tells FT
data.description:
Circle (NYSE:CRCL), the world's second-largest stablecoin issuer, is reportedly considering introducing a "reversible" mechanism for its token transactions.

Circle's president, Heath Tarbert, told the Financial Times on Thursday that the company is examining mechanisms that could allow transactions to be rolled back in cases of fraud or hacks while still maintaining settlement finality.

“We are thinking through [. . .] whether or not there’s the possibility of reversibility of transactions, right, but at the same time, we want settlement finality,” Tarbert told the FT. “So there’s an inherent tension there between being able to transfer something immediately, but having it be irrevocable [...].”

Such measures would be a major departure from the crypto industry’s previous emphasis on the “immutability” of the blockchain, a digital ledger that is public and records transactions that cannot be unwound.

Tarbert, a former chair of digital assets regulator the US Commodity Futures Trading Commission, said there were discussions taking place among software developers “as to whether on certain blockchains for certain circumstances, provided all the parties agree, there could be some degree of reversibility for fraud.”

Circle (NYSE:CRCL), in June, raised $1.1B in an upsized initial public offering, as the stablecoin issuer priced its shares above the marketed range. Its shares have soared 324% so far this year.

data.title: Crypto rout deepens as U.S. government shutdown fears mount
data.description:
Ether (ETH-USD) dropped below $4,000 to its weakest level in nearly seven weeks, deepening a digital-asset selloff that’s wiped out more than $140 billion in market value since Monday

The world’s second-largest cryptocurrency fell as much as 4.7% to $3,969 on Thursday, while Bitcoin (BTC-USD) slipped 1.7%, Bloomberg data showed.

Other notable crypto decliners included XRP (XRP-USD) -3.1%, Binance Coin (BNB-USD) -2.7%, Solana (SOL-USD) -3.5%, Stellar (XLM-USD) -3.5%, Celestia (TIA-USD) -2.3%, Uniswap (UNI-USD) -2%, Avalanche (AVAX-USD) -4.8%, and Dogecoin USD (DOGE-USD) -3.2%.

The decline came as “institutional inflows cooled” and “technical signals point to short-term pressure,” said Rachael Lucas, a crypto analyst at BTC Markets.

In addition, according to decentralized betting platform Polymarket, odds of a U.S. government shutdown hit record highs, with the price of Yes-side shares for the betting contract "U.S. government shutdown in 2025?" rising to 77%, the highest since the contract's launch in January. Traders are betting on a 77% chance that the U.S. Office of Personnel Management will declare a government shutdown from a funding lapse by Dec. 31, while the odds of a shutdown as early as Oct. 1 are pegged at 63%.

US-listed Ether exchange-traded funds have already seen nearly $300 million in outflows since Monday, when $1.7 billion in bullish bets were erased in a sudden downturn. Lucas warned that more liquidations could follow if Ether breaks below $3,800.

Ether-linked ETFs: iShares Ethereum Trust (ETHA), Grayscale Ethereum Trust (ETHE), Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust (ETH), Bitwise Ethereum ETF (ETHW), VanEck Ethereum ETF (ETHV), Fidelity Ethereum Fund (FETH), Franklin Ethereum ETF (EZET), Invesco Galaxy Ethereum ETF (QETH), and 21Shares Core Ethereum ETF (CETH).

Crypto-linked stocks were down premarket: Coinbase (COIN) fell -0.7%, Riot Platforms (RIOT) -0.7%, Marathon Digital (MARA) -0.6%, MicroStrategy (MSTR) -0.5%, Sharplink Gaming (SBET) -3.9%, Bit Digital (BTBT) -1.6%, Bullish (BLSH) -2.2% and BitMine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) -2% — the latter building a large ETH treasury and thus directly sensitive to Ether’s price swings.

data.title: Singapore orders Meta to curb Facebook impersonation scams
Biggest stock movers Thursday: RIG, IMRX, and more
data.description:
Stock futures ticked higher in the premarket hours of Thursday as investors awaited the release of key jobs data, hoping for signals on labor market strength and its potential impact on future Federal Reserve rate policy.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Immuneering (NASDAQ:IMRX) +28% – Shares surged after phase 2a data showed its atebimetinib combo (with modified gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel) delivered an 86% 9-month survival rate in first-line pancreatic cancer, nearly double the standard 47%. Median overall survival was not yet reached. Progression-free survival hit 53% vs. ~29% for standard of care. The regimen was generally well-tolerated, with Grade 3 neutropenia and anemia the main adverse events. Immuneering plans to start a phase 3 trial by year-end and aims to dose the first patient in mid-2026, while also exploring trials in lung cancer.

  • Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) +4% - Shares rose after the reports surfaced that the chipmaker has reportedly approached Apple (AAPL) about a potential investment. Discussions remain at an early stage and may not lead to action. Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan has recently secured a wave of large investments, including $2B from SoftBank (SFTBY), a 10% U.S. government stake, and a $5B infusion from Nvidia (NVDA). Apple was once a key Intel customer, adopting its processors in 2006 before shifting Macs to in-house ARM-based chips by 2023.

Biggest stock losers

  • Transocean (NYSE:RIG) -13% - Shares slumped after the offshore driller priced a public offering of 125M shares at $3.05 each, larger than the 100M shares originally planned. The deal is expected to raise about $381M, with proceeds earmarked for debt repayment.

  • Stitch Fix (NASDAQ:SFIX) -7% - Shares fell despite beating FQ4 estimates and issuing stronger-than-expected FQ1 and FY2026 guidance, as subscriber losses continued for a 15th straight quarter. Active clients dropped 7.9% Y/Y, while the amount spent per client rose 3% to $549. Gross margin narrowed 100 bps to 43.6% on higher transport costs and weaker product margins. For FQ1, sales are seen at $333M–$338M (vs. $274M consensus) with adjusted EBITDA of $8M–$11M. FY2026 revenue is guided to $1.28B–$1.33B (vs. $1.23B est.) and adjusted EBITDA to $30M–$45M, below FY2025’s $49.1M.
